BUDGET FOOD DELIVERY | Swiggy's Toing Leads Expansion

Key Highlights
- Toing expands to 50 cities
- Rapido's Only remains Bengaluru-focused
- Zomato integrates affordability
- Profitability remains the key challenge

Industry Developments
- Swiggy's Toing has expanded to 50 cities within 10 months of launch.
- The platform leverages Swiggy's existing restaurant network while offering lower platform and delivery charges.
- Rapido's Only remains limited to Bengaluru, despite earlier plans for multi-city expansion.
- Eternal (Zomato) has chosen to integrate affordability into its main app by lowering the Gold free-delivery threshold to ₹99 and expanding low-priced meal options.

Business Models
- Toing (Swiggy): Geographic expansion supported by existing restaurant partnerships.
- Only (Rapido): Zero commission for restaurants with no delivery or platform fees, focusing on hyperlocal density.
- Zomato: Affordability integrated within the core platform instead of launching a separate app.

Key Industry Insights
- Toing currently operates under Swiggy's Platform Innovations business.
- Rapido's Only reportedly processes 10,000+ daily orders in Bengaluru but continues to incur significant per-order losses.
- Analysts believe budget food delivery remains an evolving segment with profitability yet to be established.
- Lower-priced offerings may expand the customer base but also carry the risk of cannibalising premium food delivery businesses.

Key Takeaway
- India's budget food delivery market is entering a new phase of experimentation, with Swiggy, Rapido and Zomato pursuing different strategies to attract price-sensitive consumers. While Toing has taken an early lead in expansion, the industry's long-term success will ultimately depend on achieving sustainable unit economics alongside customer growth.

POWER EQUIPMENT | Chinese Firms Get Limited Market Access

Key Highlights
- Four Chinese firms receive exemption
- Two-year approval for government tenders
- Competition in power equipment to increase
- Domestic players remain confident

Policy Update
- The government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese power equipment manufacturers with manufacturing facilities in India.
- Eligible companies include TBEA Energy, Nanjing Electric India, New Northeast Electric India and Taikai Electric (India).
- The exemption allows participation in critical government power sector tenders.
- The decision follows a request from the Ministry of Power to address supply constraints in critical transmission equipment.

Industry Perspective
- Domestic manufacturers expect greater competition in future transmission project bidding.
- Industry believes project awards will continue to depend on:
  - Technology
  - Manufacturing capability
  - Product quality
  - Execution track record
  - Long-term reliability
- Indian companies highlighted their investments in capacity expansion, localisation, R&D and testing infrastructure under the Make in India initiative.

Why It Matters
- The exemption aims to ease near-term shortages of critical power equipment required for India's expanding transmission network.
- It balances immediate infrastructure requirements with the long-term objective of strengthening domestic manufacturing.

Key Takeaway
- The government's temporary two-year exemption is expected to increase competition in India's power equipment sector, while domestic manufacturers believe their engineering capabilities, localisation efforts and execution experience will help maintain competitiveness as the country accelerates investment in transmission infrastructure.

INDIA EV MARKET | Q1 FY27 Registration Update

Key Highlights
- EV registrations surged 56.6% YoY
- Penetration crossed 11% in June
- Uttar Pradesh remained the largest EV market
- Fuel price hikes accelerated adoption

Q1 FY27 Snapshot
- EV registrations increased to 8,25,452 units, up 56.6% YoY.
- Growth was driven by strong demand across two-wheelers, commercial three-wheelers and passenger vehicles.
- Monthly registrations rose from 2,48,567 in April to 3,05,566 in June, reflecting sustained momentum.

State-wise Leaders
- Uttar Pradesh: 1,17,388 registrations (14.2% market share).
- Maharashtra: 93,418 registrations (11.3% share).
- Karnataka: 76,767 registrations (9.3% share).
- Tamil Nadu: 71,463 registrations (8.7% share).
- These four states together accounted for 43.5% of India's EV registrations during Q1 FY27.

Key Trends
- EV penetration increased to 11.95% of total vehicle registrations in June.
- June registrations were 12.6% higher than May and 69.7% above the previous year.
- Rising petrol, diesel and CNG prices improved the cost advantage of EV ownership.
- In Uttar Pradesh, electric three-wheelers contributed nearly 58% of EV registrations, highlighting strong commercial adoption.

Key Takeaway
- India's EV market continued its strong growth trajectory in Q1 FY27, supported by higher fuel prices, improving economics and increasing commercial adoption. The data indicates that last-mile mobility and commercial transport remain the primary drivers of EV penetration, while leading states continue to account for a significant share of industry growth.

INDIAN GROCERY RETAIL | Rise of Value Supermarkets

Key Highlights
- Affordability over delivery speed
- Neighbourhood supermarket expansion
- Targets middle-income households
- Growing investor interest

Emerging Retail Trend
- Small-format grocery chains are expanding by focusing on value-driven, planned shopping rather than instant delivery.
- Brands such as SuperK, Apna Mart, SAVOmart, 7xBasket and Grocery4u are building neighbourhood stores across Tier-2 and Tier-3 markets.

Business Model
- Typical store size ranges from 900–1,200 sq. ft.
- Primarily caters to weekly and monthly grocery purchases.
- Targets households with monthly incomes of ₹25,000–50,000.
- Positioned between traditional kirana stores and large supermarkets.

Why Consumers Prefer the Format
- Transparent pricing.
- Wider product assortment.
- Better merchandising and shopping experience.
- Lower overall grocery bills.
- Convenient neighbourhood locations.

Value vs Quick Commerce
- Quick Commerce: Competes on speed and convenience for urgent purchases.
- Value Supermarkets: Compete on affordability, offering 5–10% savings on larger grocery baskets.
- Consumers continue to prefer kiranas for emergency purchases while using value supermarkets for planned shopping.

Expansion Trends
- SuperK: 130+ stores across Andhra Pradesh, Telangana and Karnataka.
- Apna Mart: 200+ stores across Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, West Bengal and Bihar.
- SAVOmart: Around 20 outlets since its launch in 2024.

Key Takeaway
- India's grocery retail market is witnessing the emergence of a third retail format between kirana stores and quick commerce. By prioritising affordability, assortment and neighbourhood convenience, small-format value supermarkets are attracting both price-conscious consumers and investor interest, highlighting a significant long-term opportunity in organised retail.

ICICI PRUDENTIAL LIFE | Prudential Seeks Promoter Declassification

Key Highlights
- Prudential to retain 22% stake
- Promoter declassification planned
- Board nominee to step down
- No immediate stake sale expected

Business Updates
- Prudential is expected to apply to IRDAI for promoter declassification while retaining its 22% stake in ICICI Prudential Life.
- The company is not keen to reduce its stake following the recent decline in the stock price.
- Prudential's nominee director is expected to step down from the board as part of the declassification process.
- Under IRDAI regulations, promoter declassification requires shareholding below 25% and no board representation.
- This framework allows Prudential to continue holding its 22% stake without being classified as a promoter.

Key Takeaway
- Prudential is planning a regulatory reclassification rather than a stake sale, enabling it to retain its 22% holding in ICICI Prudential Life while complying with IRDAI's promoter declassification norms.

THE DRAGON IN THE SUPPLY CHAIN – I | Electronics: Assembly Boom, Component Gap

Key Highlights
- Electronics exports hit record highs
- Smartphones dominate exports
- Component imports remain high
- Ecosystem depth is the next challenge

India's Electronics Growth
- Electronics exports reached a record ~US$48 billion in FY26.
- Smartphone exports exceeded US$31 billion, driven by global manufacturing expansion and exports to the US and Europe.
- India has become the world's second-largest mobile phone manufacturer, with production rising nearly 28x since FY15.

The Structural Challenge
- Domestic value addition in smartphones is estimated at only 18–20%.
- Integrated manufacturing ecosystems, such as China's, achieve 35–45% value addition.
- India continues to rely heavily on imported:
  - Semiconductor chips
  - Display assemblies
  - Camera modules
  - PCB components
  - Sensors and connectors
  - Manufacturing equipment
  - Specialty industrial materials

Global Perspective
- Global electronics manufacturing operates through interconnected supply chains.
- Even China imports advanced semiconductor chips from Taiwan and South Korea, while sourcing specialised equipment and materials from Japan.
- Vietnam also depends significantly on imported electronic components.
- The key differentiator is the depth of domestic manufacturing capabilities.

What India Needs
- Semiconductor fabrication and packaging
- Display manufacturing
- Electronic component ecosystem
- Advanced materials and manufacturing equipment
- Higher domestic value addition across the supply chain

Key Takeaway
- India's electronics industry has successfully emerged as a global assembly and export hub, but the next phase of growth depends on developing a deeper domestic component ecosystem. Building capabilities in semiconductors, displays, electronics components and advanced manufacturing will be essential for increasing value addition, reducing import dependence and strengthening long-term competitiveness.

Mumbai real estate is quietly telling an interesting story.

H1 2026 turned out to be the strongest first half for Mumbai’s housing market since 2013 — both in terms of volumes and revenue collections.

June 2026 alone saw 13,302 property registrations, up 15% YoY.

What stands out is that the growth does not look purely speculative.

Average city-wide prices are now moving in a more stable range of 5–6% annual appreciation, with average prices around ₹38,600 per sq ft.

So, the broader market seems to be moving from sharp price spikes to a more fundamentals-led cycle.

In this backdrop, a few company-level numbers become interesting to track.

Hubtown reported in FY26:
Proforma pre-sales of ₹4,382 crore
Cash collections of ₹1,910 crore
Unrecognized revenue pipeline of ₹11,365 crore

For FY27, management has guided for:
Pre-sales of ₹6,000 crore
Cash collections of ₹3,000 crore

For comparison, Lodha Developers has guided for ₹24,000 crore of pre-sales in FY27, implying around 17% growth over FY26.

The scale is very different.
But the direction is similar: higher launches, higher collections, and stronger revenue visibility.

For Hubtown, the next few quarters become important.

The company has seen strong Q1 sales momentum in Mumbai, good progress in 25 Downtown, strong sales in 25 West, and the launch of the balance 25 Series projects.

Another key monitorable is the expected OC receipt for the North Tower in 25 South.

If these timelines move as expected, Hubtown may be able to recognise revenue from projects like North Tower, Rising City, and Seasons, using part of its large unrecognized revenue pipeline.

So, the story is not just about guidance.

It is about whether sales, collections, OC approvals, and revenue recognition start moving together.

That is where Hubtown becomes an interesting execution-led real estate story to track in Mumbai’s current housing cycle.

SEMICONDUCTOR THEME | 4 Indirect Beneficiaries

Key Highlights
- Software enables smart chips
- Specialty chemicals are essential
- Ultra-pure water is critical
- High-grade piping supports fabrication

Theme Overview

IZMO
- Provides automotive software solutions.
- Modern vehicles require software to integrate and optimize semiconductor functionality.

Aether Industries
- Manufactures high-purity specialty chemicals.
- Supplies critical chemical intermediates used in semiconductor manufacturing processes.

VA Tech Wabag
- Designs ultra-pure water treatment and recycling systems.
- Semiconductor fabrication plants require large volumes of highly purified water for production.

Venus Pipes
- Manufactures premium stainless steel pipes and tubing.
- Semiconductor facilities require corrosion-resistant piping to safely transport ultra-pure water and specialty chemicals.

Why These Companies Matter
- Semiconductor manufacturing extends far beyond chip fabrication.
- A successful chip ecosystem depends on software, specialty chemicals, water infrastructure and industrial piping.
- These businesses provide enabling technologies and infrastructure that support semiconductor manufacturing.

Key Takeaway
- The semiconductor opportunity is broader than chip manufacturers alone. Companies such as IZMO, Aether Industries, VA Tech Wabag and Venus Pipes represent indirect beneficiaries, supplying the critical technologies and infrastructure that every semiconductor manufacturing facility requires.

Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.
Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out
Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up
Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

*CLSA on Industrials*
Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.
More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains
Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power
Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

FADA | June 2026 Retail Sales Report

Key Highlights
- Overall retail sales up 21.8% YoY
- Best-ever June for key vehicle segments
- EV penetration reaches new highs
- Festive season outlook remains positive

Retail Sales Snapshot
- Overall registrations at 25.57 lakh units, up 21.8% YoY and 1.0% MoM.
- Two-Wheelers: 18.28 lakh units (+21.2% YoY).
- Passenger Vehicles: 4.11 lakh units (+28.6% YoY).
- Commercial Vehicles: 90,972 units (+16.9% YoY).
- Three-Wheelers: 1.21 lakh units (+16.2% YoY).
- Tractors: 1.01 lakh units (+25.3% YoY), second-best June ever.
- Construction Equipment: 5,244 units (-40.9% YoY).

Key Industry Trends
- Alternative-fuel passenger vehicles (CNG, Hybrid & EV) crossed 40% market share for the first time.
- EV penetration in two-wheelers reached 10.6%, entering double digits for the first time.
- Urban demand remained stronger, while rural demand softened sequentially due to delayed monsoon.
- Lower crude oil prices supported vehicle supplies, while June price hikes had limited impact on bookings.
- Passenger vehicle inventory increased to 32–34 days, above FADA's recommended 21-day level.

Outlook
- 51.2% of dealers expect sales growth in July 2026.
- 66.2% of dealers expect growth during July–September, supported by the festive season.
- Key risks include monsoon shortfall, El Niño, price hikes, financing challenges and rural demand weakness.

Key Takeaway
- FADA's June data reflects broad-based strength across India's automobile market, with record retail sales, rising alternative-fuel adoption and improving dealer optimism. However, higher inventory levels and rural demand remain important monitorables ahead of the festive season.

CLSA on Industrials

Indian government has granted a two-year exemption to four Chinese electrical equipment manufacturers with manufacturing units in India, allowing them to participate in government/CPSE tenders.

More important is that this exemption was granted at request of Ministry of Power, validating case led by complaints from transmission utilities struggling with price inflation on controlled supply chains

Do not see this move impacting near-term volume growth, but increased competition (ex-HVDC converters) is likely to weigh on pricing power & margins of domestic T&D equipment cos such as Hitachi, GE Vernova T&D, BHEL (U-PF) & CG Power

Investors should be wary of lofty valuations (PE of 50-90x) on historic peak high margins led by ‘Make in India” driven shield but now govt seems to have taken cognisance of excessive margins being made by transformer sector.

Macquarie on Industrials

As per industry sources, government is set to exempt 4 Chinese players (detailed below), having manufacturing units in India, from the restriction imposed in February 2023 for critical power projects.

This exemption is valid for a period of two years (till June 2028)

Most of grid companies are in midst of capacity expansion and are likely to add capacity over the next 2-3 year, which is the validity of this exemption.

Should this exemption be to extend in future, long-term negative impact on margins of the incumbents, especially the 765kV GIS players cannot be ruled out

Barring TBEA, most of other companies have sub-scale operations in India & are likely to take time to ramp up

Believe this exemption is to address current shortage of certain of EHV grid equipment & unlikely to allow large scale Chinese imports in sensitive areas of grid infrastructure.

Overreaction to narrow exemption

Order explicitly states it creates no precedent

Market players read it as a competitive threat & sold off listed power-equipment stocks such as - GE Vernova T&D India (Buy), CG Power (Buy), Hitachi Energy Not rated) & Siemens Energy Not rated)

Exempted players won 9% of transmission-linked PGCIL tenders over FY09-20

Uneven capacity readiness across four entities: only one appears to be running near-optimal utilization

Exemption is explicitly sunset-bound, & grid infrastructure sits squarely in national-security bucket

Two-year window is too short to underwrite meaningful capacity expansion in India

According to Morgan Stanley Research, Asia is likely to invest $5.5 trillion in energy over the next five years as governments and companies work to strengthen supply chains, support economic growth and meet rising power demand.

“We are at a critical inflection point where energy, AI and security converge into a once-in-a-generation investment cycle,” says Mayank Maheshwari, who leads Morgan Stanley’s energy and utilities coverage in India and Southeast Asia. “This is likely to be the largest and longest energy investment cycle in history, with implications across every asset class, sector and geography.”

For investors, this increase in energy capital expenditure could create as much as $9 trillion in opportunities across the value chain, spanning industries from power generation and infrastructure to fertilizers and materials critical to artificial intelligence.

“While Asia is unlikely to become fully energy independent, this investment cycle can reduce reliance on concentrated supply sources and diversify both import partners and fuel types,” Maheshwari says.

U.S. DOJ: ADANI CASE SHOULD NEVER HAVE BEEN FILED; SAYS U.S. SHOULD NOT ACT AS ‘WORLD POLICE’

The U.S. Department of Justice has told a federal court that the criminal case against Gautam Adani should never have been brought, arguing it involved predominantly foreign conduct with limited U.S. jurisdiction, little chance of success, and no direct impact on U.S. national security or companies. The DOJ also said the U.S. should not serve as the “world police” in such cases and has asked the court to permanently dismiss the charges.

WEEKLY INSIGHTS | Pharma, IT & Investing Lessons

1️⃣ Gufic Biosciences – Execution Phase Begins

Key Highlights
- Indore capex completed
- Commercialisation phase begins
- Complex injectables focus
- Strong innovation-led platform

Why It Stands Out
- End-to-end capabilities in lyophilized injectables, liquids, ampoules, microspheres and NDDS under one roof.
- Transitioning from a distributor-led export model to an IP-owned, marketing authorisation-driven global strategy.
- Large manufacturing scale (100K+ vial batches) offers meaningful cost advantages for exports.
- No major greenfield capex planned over the next two years, allowing focus to shift towards utilisation, execution and profitability.
- International approvals continue to expand across Europe, South Africa and other regulated markets.
- Validation work underway for a GLP-1 lyophilised vial CDMO programme.
- Management expects operating leverage as plant utilisation improves over the coming years.

Key Takeaway
- Gufic appears to be moving from an investment cycle into an execution cycle, where future value creation will depend on capacity utilisation, global product launches and successful commercial execution rather than additional capital expenditure.

---

2️⃣ Coforge – AI as a Growth Enabler

Key Highlights
- Strong Investor Day presentation
- AI viewed as a growth driver
- Clear long-term strategy
- Strong management confidence

Why It Stands Out
- Management views AI as an opportunity to expand services rather than compress revenues.
- Focus remains on integrating AI across delivery, client solutions and internal operations.
- Detailed business-unit presentations provide strong insights into future growth areas and industry trends.
- Leadership continues to communicate with high clarity on execution priorities.

Key Takeaway
- Coforge's messaging suggests that AI can become a demand accelerator for high-quality IT services firms, with execution and client adoption likely to determine long-term winners.

---

3️⃣ Investing Lesson – Research Alone Isn't Enough

Key Highlights
- Research is only one part
- Execution drives returns
- Position sizing matters
- Conviction creates wealth

Key Takeaway
- Successful investing depends not only on finding quality businesses, but also on buying them at sensible valuations, sizing positions appropriately, having conviction through market cycles, and remaining patient. Research identifies opportunities, while disciplined execution determines long-term outcomes.

Global Semiconductor Supply Chain Remains Highly Concentrated

Key Highlights:

🏭 TSMC controls over 80% of the world's leading-edge (<16nm) chip production, powering most AI servers and premium smartphones.

🌏 Taiwan, South Korea, and China account for ~85% of global semiconductor foundry revenue, highlighting regional concentration.

💾 Samsung and SK Hynix produce ~70% of the world's DRAM memory chips.

⚙️ ASML holds a near-100% monopoly in EUV lithography, the critical technology required to manufacture chips below 7nm.

🇺🇸 US companies generate around 60% of global chip design revenue, while China accounts for ~38% of global OSAT (chip packaging and testing) capacity.

🏗️ 56% of global wafer capacity still lies in mature nodes (≥34nm), creating an opportunity for emerging semiconductor manufacturing hubs like India.

text:
Bernstein on Trent

O-P, TP cut to Rs 3500 from Rs 5000

While Zudio has overshadowed Westside in store adds over last 5+ years, Westside is still 40% of Trent’s total retail area & surprised on store

additions in F26 after lackluster F23-F25 years

Proprietary store data indicates 3 things for Westside

(1) Store rationalization is over and helped Trent move away from certain cities & markets,

(2) Stores additions indicate focus in Top-100 cities only with city-densification underway

(3) Pin-densification is not visible (unlike what Trent did with Zudio)

Believe approx +50 stores/year is an achievable target for next 4-5 years, which will take them to ~500-550 stores.

For context, Pantaloons has 400 may add 20/year.

Hence, do not see whitespace as an issue and with a clearer strategy, expect them to achieve this target.

CLSA on NHPC

High Conviction O-P, TP Rs 117

NDR takeaways,

Key message from NHPC was FY26 saw start of two of its largest projects, driving regulated equity (RegEq) up 25% YoY, but a delay in Parbati 2 (P2) tariff & restoration of Teesta deflated PAT by c.20%.

Management addressed clients’ biggest concerns about PAT growth by clarifying its conservative accounting policy has deflated FY26 PAT, but once it secures final tariff for P2 HEP, full PAT will be recognised retrospectively with interest, so there is no loss of value on an NPV basis.

NHPC is biggest beneficiary of Indus water treaty being put in abeyance and it has awarded two of the four hydro projects already, which are sub four-year projects

Forecast REE (ex-minority) growth of 90% over FY26-30CL versus 31% growth over past five years

With a step-change in REE, forecast its EPS to double & its ROE to expand 750bps over FY26-28CL.

Atharva Polyplast looks more than just a plastic component maker.
What stands out is its end-to-end manufacturing setup — from design and tooling to moulding, finishing, and assembly — all under one roof.

A few things make the story interesting:

Strong compliance stack helps it qualify for global customers
Margins have improved sharply, driven by a better product mix
Engineering services can become a meaningful third growth engine
The company has scale-up headroom, backed by land, capacity, and premium market opportunity

In simple words, Atharva seems to be positioning itself as a precision manufacturing partner for global players, not just a commodity supplier.

If execution stays on track, this could be a business worth tracking closely.
